title: cellNexus
text: cellNexus extends functionality CuratedAtlasQueryR providing unified interface querying accessing harmonised, curated, reannotated CELLxGENE human cell atlas. enables reproducible, programmatic exploration large-scale single-cell datasets, supporting data retrieval cell, sample, dataset levels flexible filtering based tissue, cell type, experimental condition, metadata. Retrieved data returned formats ready downstream analysis. package integrates 44 million human cells processed standardised pipeline, including consistent quality control, normalisation, unified abundance representations (e.g., single-cell, counts-per-million, normalised expression, pseudobulk). harmonisation facilitates efficient cross-dataset comparison integration. Data hosted ARDC Nectar Research Cloud, functions access via web requests; therefore, active network connection required typical use. cellNexus CuratedAtlasQueryR rely precomputed expression layers, cellNexus adopts standardised transparent processing workflow. includes explicit removal empty droplets dead cells, followed harmonised quality control, normalisation, multi-layer data generation, ensuring alignment evolving CELLxGENE releases.   plot chunk fig-logo   plot chunk fig-funders   plot chunk fig-funders   plot chunk fig-funders   plot chunk fig-funders   plot chunk fig-funders   plot chunk fig-funders

title: cellNexus
text: Cell communication metadata generated based post-QC cells per sample using CellChat v2 method. uses harmonised cell type annotation (cell_type_unified_ensemble) infer communication. captures inferred communication ligand–receptor pair level signalling pathway level. interaction_count: number inferred interactions pair cell groups. interaction_weight: aggregated communication strength pair cell groups. definitions additional annotations, please refer CellChat v2 documentation: https://github.com/jinworks/CellChat. demonstration purpose, read cell communication metadata demo file . Users need specify cloud_metadata argument case.

title: cellNexus
text: returned SingleCellExperiment can saved three modalities, .rds HDF5 H5AD.

what: Saving as RDS (fast saving, slow reading)
title: cellNexus
text: Saving .rds advantage fast, .rds file occupies little disk space stores links files cache. However disadvantage big SingleCellExperiment objects, merge lot HDF5 get_single_cell_experiment, display manipulation going slow. addition, .rds saved way portable: able share users.
code: single_cell_counts |>   saveRDS("single_cell_counts.rds")

what: Saving as HDF5 (slow saving, fast reading)
title: cellNexus
text: Saving .hdf5 executes computation SingleCellExperiment writes disk monolithic HDF5. done, operations SingleCellExperiment comparatively fast. resulting .hdf5 file also totally portable sharable. However .hdf5 disadvantage larger corresponding .rds includes copy count information, saving process going slow large objects.
code: # ! IMPORTANT if you save 200K+ cells HDF5Array::setAutoBlockSize(size = 1e+09)  single_cell_counts |>   HDF5Array::saveHDF5SummarizedExperiment(     "single_cell_counts",     replace = TRUE,     as.sparse = TRUE,     verbose = TRUE   )

what: Saving as H5AD (slow saving, fast reading)
title: cellNexus
text: Saving .h5ad executes computation SingleCellExperiment writes disk monolithic H5AD. H5AD format HDF5 disk representation AnnData object well-supported Python. However .h5ad saving strategy bottleneck handling columns NA values SingleCellExperiment metadata.
code: single_cell_counts |>   anndataR::write_h5ad("single_cell_counts.h5ad",     compression = "gzip",     verbose = TRUE   )

title: cellNexus
text: cellNexus enables users query metadata also allows integration local metadata. Additionally, users can integrate metadata stored cloud. enable feature, users must include file_id_cellNexus_single_cell atlas_id (e.g cellxgene/dd-mm-yy) columns metadata. See metadata structure cellNexus::pbmc3k_sce
code: # Set up local cache and paths local_cache <- tempdir() layer <- "counts" meta_path <- file.path(local_cache, "pbmc3k_metadata.parquet") data(pbmc3k_sce)  # Extract and prepare metadata pbmc3k_metadata <- pbmc3k_sce |>   S4Vectors::metadata() |>   purrr::pluck("data") |>   dplyr::mutate(     counts_directory = file.path(tempdir(), atlas_id, layer),     sce_path = file.path(counts_directory, file_id_cellNexus_single_cell)   )  # Get unique paths counts_directory <- pbmc3k_metadata |>   dplyr::pull(counts_directory) |>   unique()  sce_path <- pbmc3k_metadata |>   dplyr::pull(sce_path) |>   unique()  # Create directory structure dir.create(counts_directory, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  # Save data to disk pbmc3k_sce |>   S4Vectors::metadata() |>   purrr::pluck("data") |>   arrow::write_parquet(meta_path)  # Save SCE object pbmc3k_sce |>   anndataR::write_h5ad(sce_path, compression = "gzip", mode = "w") # A cellNexus file file_id_from_cloud <- "e52795dec7b626b6276b867d55328d9f___1.h5ad" file_id_local <- basename(sce_path)  get_metadata(   cloud_metadata = cellNexus::SAMPLE_DATABASE_URL,   local_metadata = meta_path,   cache_directory = local_cache ) |>   # For illustration purpose, only filter a selected cloud metadata and the saved metadata   dplyr::filter(file_id_cellNexus_single_cell %in% c(file_id_from_cloud, file_id_local)) |>   dplyr::select(cell_id, sample_id, dataset_id, cell_type_unified_ensemble, atlas_id, file_id_cellNexus_single_cell) |>   get_single_cell_experiment(cache_directory = local_cache) #> ℹ Realising metadata. #> ℹ Synchronising files #> ℹ Reading files. #> ℹ Compiling Experiment. #> class: SingleCellExperiment  #> dim: 13132 500  #> metadata(1): data #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(13132): ENSG00000228463 ENSG00000228327 ... title: cellNexus
text: counts assay represents RNA abundance positive real scale, without non-linear transformations (e.g., log square root). original CELLxGENE data, values provided using mix scales transformations. method required invert transformations recorded inverse_transform column. cpm assay includes counts per million. sct assay includes normalised counts sctranform.

title: cellNexus

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
text: cellNexus query interface programmatic exploration retrieval harmonised, curated, reannotated CELLxGENE human-cell-atlas data.  standalone documentation website provides: Detailed data-processing information (quality control, harmonisation, expression representations). Complete explanations metadata columns used filtering interpretation. Guided examples metadata-first exploration gene-expression analysis.

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
text: harmonisation pipeline standardises data across datasets queries consistent across studies: Metadata retrieved cloud-hosted harmonised tables. Standardised quality control removes empty droplets, dead/damaged cells, likely doublets. Cell-level data served common assay layers (counts, cpm, sct, pseudobulk). Outputs returned analysis-ready R formats SingleCellExperiment Seurat.

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
text: QC flags used throughout cellNexus computed using HPCell. brief: Empty droplets (empty_droplet): Computed SingleCellExperiment. Excludes mitochondrial genes ribosomal genes scoring: Computes, per cell, number expressed genes flags cell empty droplet (n_ <) RNA_feature_threshold (default 200, except targeted panels Rhapsody technology). Alive cells (alive): Filters empty droplets. Computes per-cell QC metrics raw counts using scuttle::perCellQCMetrics(..., subsets=list(Mito=...)), mitochondrial subset defined ^MT feature names. Determines high mitochondrial content via scater::isOutlier(subsets_Mito_percent, type="higher"). Outlier calling performed within cell-type group using harmonised label cell_type_unified_ensemble. Alive cells labelled without high mitochondrial content (!high_mitochondrion). Doublets (scDblFinder.class): Filters empty droplets. scDblFinder::scDblFinder() default parameters used. cells classified scDblFinder, class set "Unknown" avoid dropping cells.

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
text: RNA count distributions per sample annotated cellxgenedp, using x_approximate_distribution column. Counts-per-million normalisation computed raw counts assay via scuttle::calculateCPM(). Per-cell gene-expression ranks computed singscore::rankGenes(). Implemented column chunks (default 1000 cells per slice) handle large datasets; slices written disk HDF5Array-backed sparse integer matrix column-bound. Variance-stabilising normalisation computed Seurat SCTransform() (v2), regression cell-level covariates (subsets_Mito_percent subsets_Ribo_percent). QC filtering applied first. median common scale across whole resource applied (scale_factor=2186) low-quality cells flagged QC removed aggregation. Aggregates counts across cells using scuttle::aggregateAcrossCells(), aggregating sample_id harmonised cell type (cell_type_harmonised_ensemble).

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
text: harmonisation curation, cellNexus adds columns present original CELLxGENE metadata alone. Field definitions CELLxGENE schema follow CELLxGENE schema 5.1.0, CELLxGENE Census schema

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
code: library(cellNexus) library(dplyr) library(stringr)  metadata <- get_metadata() |>   join_census_table()  metadata <- metadata |>   keep_quality_cells()  query <- metadata |>   filter(     self_reported_ethnicity == "African",     str_like(assay, "%10x%"),     tissue == "lung parenchyma",     str_like(cell_type, "%CD4%")   )  sce <- get_single_cell_experiment(query, assays = c("counts", "cpm")) pb <- get_pseudobulk(query)

title: Queries the Human Cell Atlas
text: Python support available companion repository: MangiolaLaboratory/cellNexusPy. implementation details code examples, see cellNexus README
code: from cellnexuspy import get_metadata, get_anndata  sample_dataset = "https://object-store.rc.nectar.org.au/v1/AUTH_06d6e008e3e642da99d806ba3ea629c5/cellNexus-metadata/sample_metadata.1.3.0.parquet" conn, table = get_metadata(parquet_url=sample_dataset)  table = table.filter("""     empty_droplet = 'false'     AND alive = 'true'     AND "scDblFinder.class" != 'doublet'     AND feature_count >= 5000 """)  query = table.filter("""     self_reported_ethnicity = 'African'     AND assay LIKE '%10%'     AND tissue = 'lung parenchyma'     AND cell_type LIKE '%CD4%' """)  adata = get_anndata(query, assay="cpm") pb = get_anndata(query, aggregation="pseudobulk") conn.close()

title: Gets a SingleCellExperiment from curated metadata — get_SingleCellExperiment
text: ... Arguments passed get_single_cell_experiment data data frame containing, minimum, cell_id, file_id_cellNexus_single_cell atlas_id columns, correspond single cell ID, file subdivision internal use, atlas name format (e.g cellxgene_2024/0.1.0) internal use. can obtained get_metadata() function. Use get_atlas_versions() download atlas versions data frame. assays character vector specifying desired assay(s) requested. Valid elements include "counts", "cpm", "rank", "sct" single-cell analyses default setting retrieves counts assay. analysis involves smaller set genes, consider using "cpm" assay. "rank" assay suited signature calculations across millions cells. cell_aggregation character vector specifies cell aggregation strategy applied. create corresponding subdirectory cache directory. Single cell level applied default. cache_directory optional character vector length one. provided, indicate local file path remotely accessed files copied. repository character vector length one. provided, HTTP URL pointing location single cell data stored. features optional character vector features (ie genes) return counts . default counts features returned.

title: Retrieve cellNexus cell communication ligand–receptor strength as a data frame. — get_cell_communication_strength
text: returned table integrates three levels cell communication inference CellChat, sample: () ligand–receptor–level communication (lr_prob, lr_pval), (ii) pathway-level aggregated signaling (pathway_prob, pathway_pval), (iii) cell-pair–level summaries communication breadth (interaction_count - number significant LR interactions) intensity (interaction_weight - overall communication strength). title: Gets the CellNexus metadata as a data frame. — get_metadata
text: metadata collected Bioconductor package cellxgenedp. vignette("using_cellxgenedp", package="cellxgenedp") provides overview columns metadata. data column organism_name included "Homo sapiens" collected collected cellxgenedp. columns dataset_id file_id_cellNexus_single_cell link datasets explorable cellNexus cellxgenedpto CELLxGENE portal. representation, harmonises metadata dataset, sample cell levels, unique coherent database table. Field definitions CELLxGENE schema follow CELLxGENE schema 5.1.0. harmonisation curation introduced custom columns present original CELLxGENE metadata: cell_count: Number cells dataset. feature_count: Number genes dataset. age_days: Donor age days. tissue_groups: Coarse tissue grouping analysis. empty_droplet: Whether cell called empty droplet expressed-gene count per sample (default threshold 200; targeted panels may differ). alive: Whether cell passes viability / mitochondrial QC. scDblFinder.class: Doublet, singlet, unknown (scDblFinder default parameters). cell_type_unified_ensemble: Consensus immune identity Azimuth SingleR (Blueprint, Monaco). cell_annotation_azimuth_l2: Azimuth cell annotation. cell_annotation_blueprint_singler: SingleR annotation (Blueprint). cell_annotation_blueprint_monaco: SingleR annotation (Monaco). is_immune: Whether cell immune cell. sample_heuristic: Internal sample subdivision helper. file_id_cellNexus_single_cell: Internal file id single-cell layers. file_id_cellNexus_pseudobulk: Internal file id pseudobulk layers. sample_id: Harmonised sample identifier. nCount_RNA: Total RNA counts per cell (sample-aware). nFeature_expressed_in_sample: Number expressed features per cell. ethnicity_flagging_score: Supporting score ethnicity imputation. low_confidence_ethnicity: Supporting flag low-confidence ethnicity calls. .aggregated_cells: Post-QC cells combined pseudobulk sample. imputed_ethnicity: Imputed ethnicity label. atlas_id: cellNexus atlas release identifier (internal use). fields definitions, please refer documentation site Possible cache path issues default R cache path includes non-standard characters (e.g. dash user organisation name), following error can occur.   solution choose different cache, example

title: Organize arbitrary Shiny inputs into a grid layout — organize_inputs
text: Shiny tagList inputs organized grid, optionally nested inside tabsetPanel.

title: Organize arbitrary Shiny inputs into a grid layout — organize_inputs
text: Jared Andrews

title: Organize arbitrary Shiny inputs into a grid layout — organize_inputs
code:
library(shiny) # Example 1: Basic usage with a simple grid ui.inputs <- tagList( textInput("name", "Name"), numericInput("age", "Age", value = 30), selectInput("gender", "Gender", choices = c("Male", "Female", "Other")) ) organize_inputs(ui.inputs, columns = 2, rows = 2) #> [[1]] #>

title: Sample SingleCellExperiment Object — pbmc3k_sce
text: sample SingleCellExperiment object created pbmc3k dataset testing demonstration purposes. dataset contains 500 cells gene expression data mapped Ensembl gene IDs formatted cellNexus-compatible metadata structure.

title: Sample SingleCellExperiment Object — pbmc3k_sce
text: object class SingleCellExperiment : assays Gene expression matrix Ensembl gene IDs rownames colData Cell metadata including sample_id, cell_type_unified_ensemble, nCount_RNA, etc. metadata List containing 'data' field cellNexus-formatted metadata including: cell_id: Unique cell identifier sample_id: Sample identifier cell_type_unified_ensemble: Cell type annotation nCount_RNA: Number RNA molecules per cell ident: Seurat cluster identity dataset_id: Dataset identifier file_id_cellNexus_single_cell: Generated file ID cellNexus atlas_id: Atlas identifier date
